expectations.text: Uses canvas.tokens.controlled[0] for caster token (correct console context)
expectations.evidence: `const attacker = canvas.tokens.controlled[0];` in the main script.

expectations.text: Uses game.user.targets for target token (not args or workflow)
expectations.evidence: `const target = game.user.targets.first();` in the main script.

expectations.text: Includes null-check or validation on token references before running the effect
expectations.evidence: Guard clauses: `if (!attacker) { ui.notifications.error(...); throw new Error('No token selected'); }` and matching check for target.

expectations.text: Uses .waitUntilFinished() to chain projectile into impact
expectations.evidence: `.waitUntilFinished(-500)` with comment: 'start the impact slightly before the bolt ends'.

expectations.text: Uses JB2A database paths (jb2a.*) not file system paths (modules/jb2a_patreon/...)
JB2A paths reference ice/cold/frost-themed effects (not fire or generic)
expectations.evidence: ray_of_frost.blue (ice beam) and impact.frost.blue.01 (frost impact). Both explicitly ice/frost themed.

claims.claim: Provides detailed explanations of what each Sequencer method does
claims.evidence: Dedicated 'What each piece does' section explaining .file(), .atLocation(), .stretchTo(), .waitUntilFinished(), .scaleToObject().

claims.claim: Mentions integration into Midi-QOL onUse macro
claims.evidence: Last section mentions 'A Midi-QOL onUse macro attached to the Frostbite Blade weapon item' but does not provide migration code.

eval_feedback.overall: Baseline passes all 7 assertions. This eval does not discriminate strongly between with_skill and without_skill — the API patterns tested are well-known enough that both approaches get them right. The null-check assertion is the only differentiator.
